moduleGuides.title: 1. Accessing the system
moduleGuides.purpose: Log in, complete OTP if enabled, change a forced password, and confirm your role sees the correct menus.
steps: Open the internal system URL in Chrome, Edge, or Firefox.
Sign in with the username or email and password issued by ICT.
Enter the OTP code if your account has OTP enabled.
Complete forced password change when prompted before continuing.
Confirm your landing page and visible menus match your assigned role and organisation scope.
tips: If login fails, confirm the account is active and approved.
Missing menus usually mean role permissions or organisation scope — not a system fault.
Never share passwords, OTP codes, or administrator accounts.

moduleGuides.title: 2. Home page, navigation, and workflow inbox
moduleGuides.purpose: Use the ribbon menu, home dashboard, and pending workflow items.
steps: Use the ribbon to open Dashboard, Finance, Projects, Planning, Data, Procurement, Monitoring, Reports, Public, and Admin areas.
On the home page, review summary cards and any pending approvals or workflow tasks assigned to you.
Open a pending item from the inbox to approve, review, or complete the required action.
Use search and filters on list screens before concluding a record is missing.
Read success and error messages after saving, importing, or submitting records.
tips: Clear filters before reporting missing data.
The three-dot menu (top right) includes Help & Support.
For wide tables, browser zoom around 90%–110% improves readability.

moduleGuides.title: 3. AI Assistant and professional reports
moduleGuides.purpose: Ask questions about workflows, live project data, and generate downloadable Word/PDF reports from the current screen.
steps: Click the sparkle AI Assistant button at the bottom-right of any authenticated page.
Ask a question in chat — the assistant uses live data when available for your account scope.
For navigation questions (for example how to verify a certificate), it uses this Help & Support manual.
To create a formal document, click Generate Professional Report in the AI panel.
Choose report type, Word or PDF, and add instructions — the report uses data from the screen you are on.
Review AI output before official use; it is advisory and must be checked by responsible officers.
tips: Open the relevant dashboard or project screen first so the AI has the right context.
Finance dashboards produce finance-focused reports; monitoring screens produce monitoring-focused reports.
Super Admins can review AI usage under Admin > AI Usage.

moduleGuides.title: 4. Dashboards and executive views
moduleGuides.audience: Leadership, department heads, M&E, finance, and report users
moduleGuides.purpose: Review county performance through dedicated dashboards — each dashboard has a different focus.
steps: Open Dashboard from the ribbon and choose the dashboard you need.
Project By Status — counts and charts by implementation status.
Project By Sector — sector/programme distribution.
Finance Dashboard — budget, paid, absorption, and finance KPIs.
Operations Dashboard — operational delivery and attention indicators.
Jobs & Impact — employment and beneficiary impact metrics.
Summary Statistics — county-wide summary cards and trends.
Regional Breakdown — subcounty/ward distribution.
Departmental Reports — department-level summaries.
GIS Dashboard and Project GIS Map — location-based views.
Personal Dashboard (home) — your scoped summary and workflow inbox.
Set filters (department, status, sector, ward, financial year) before interpreting figures.
Use drill-down links to open related project lists where available.
tips: Dashboard totals depend on project visibility and data completeness.
Compare dashboard filters with Projects Registry filters if totals look inconsistent.
Use the AI Assistant on a dashboard to summarize that dashboard's data or generate a professional report.

moduleGuides.title: 5. Projects Registry and implementation plans
moduleGuides.audience: Project officers, departments, M&E, finance, procurement, administrators
moduleGuides.purpose: Find, create, edit, and export projects; manage implementation plans.
steps: Open Projects > Projects Registry.
Search or filter by department, financial year, sector, status, subcounty, or ward.
Open project details before editing to understand the full record.
Use Projects > Implementation Plans for cross-project implementation planning views.
Create a project only after confirming it does not already exist.
Export after applying the required filters.
tips: Search before creating duplicates.
Project visibility affects dashboards, reports, certificates, and public publishing.

moduleGuides.title: 6. Project details, evaluation, and evidence
moduleGuides.purpose: Maintain milestones, status, documents, photos, teams, partners, funding, certificates, and evaluations.
steps: Open a project from the Projects Registry.
Review the overview and tabs before adding related records.
Update milestones and status using verified implementation evidence.
Upload documents with clear names and correct categories.
Use the Certificates tab to create interim/final payment certificates (where authorised).
Use Project Evaluation Workbench (Projects menu) for structured evaluation workflows.
Link planning activities and risks so monitoring and reports stay consistent.
tips: Certificate PDFs include a QR code for public verification.
Evidence should support reported progress percentages.
Keep documents organised — audits and reports depend on them.

moduleGuides.title: 7. Planning (CIDP, ADP, RRI, traceability)
moduleGuides.audience: Planning, M&E, department focal persons, administrators
moduleGuides.purpose: Maintain planning structures, ADP implementation, RRI programmes, and budget traceability.
steps: Open Planning from the ribbon.
Maintain CIDP periods, ADP periods, sectors, programmes, and budget allocations.
Use ADP Implementation to track wishlist vs budgeted vs registered projects.
Use RRI Programmes for ward livelihood and RRI tracking.
Use Budget Traceability to follow budget lines through implementation.
Maintain indicators, activities, risks, and reporting frequency catalogues.
Link catalogues to projects for consistent monitoring and reporting.
tips: Catalogue changes affect reports — coordinate with M&E and ICT.
Search before creating new sectors, programmes, or risks.

moduleGuides.title: 8. Data import, beneficiaries, and upload logs
moduleGuides.audience: ICT admins, migration users, authorised data officers
moduleGuides.purpose: Import projects/budgets/beneficiaries safely and review upload logs.
steps: Open Data > Import Data and choose the correct import type.
Download and fill the official template without changing required column names.
Upload for preview and correct all validation errors before confirming.
Open Data Import Logs to verify completion.
Use Data > Beneficiary Registry to search and maintain beneficiary records.
tips: Never import production data from an unverified spreadsheet.
Keep original and corrected files for audit reference.

moduleGuides.title: 9. Procurement and contractors
moduleGuides.purpose: Track procurement stages, budget intake, awards, and contractor registry.
steps: Open Procurement > Project Procurement for active procurement cases.
Use Budget Procurement Intake for budget-linked procurement items.
Review Procured Projects for completed history.
Maintain Contractor Registry and contractor types.
Contractors can sign in to the Contractor Dashboard for assigned project views.
tips: Stage updates must match approved procurement documentation.
Attach evidence to support audit review.

moduleGuides.title: 10. Monitoring, PMC, and field data collection
moduleGuides.audience: M&E officers, project officers, ward teams, supervisors
moduleGuides.purpose: Record monitoring visits, PMC ward reports, accountability, checklists, and mobile field collection.
steps: Open Monitoring > Project Monitoring for structured project monitoring records.
Use PMC Ward Reports for ward-level PMC reporting.
Use Ward Accountability for accountability tracking workflows.
Open Monitoring > Checklists & visits (or Data collection tools) to manage checklist templates and visit submissions.
Download the Mobile Field Collector from Dashboard or Monitoring > Mobile app (Android).
Field staff sign in on the app, sync checklists, select a project, and submit visits offline when needed.
tips: Monitoring records should be timely and linked to the correct project.
Mobile app sync requires network — pull down to refresh checklists after login.
Escalate persistent delays, low absorption, or unresolved complaints.

moduleGuides.title: 11. Finance, payment certificates, and verification
moduleGuides.audience: Finance users, accounts, project officers, leadership, public verifiers
moduleGuides.purpose: Review finance performance, manage payment certificates, and verify certificate authenticity.
steps: Open Finance Dashboard for high-level financial KPIs.
Use Payment List for payment records and filters.
Use Payment Certificates (Finance menu) for county-wide certificate records and workflow.
On a project, open the Certificates tab to create interim/final certificates and generate PDFs.
Generated certificate PDFs include a QR code labelled Scan to verify this certificate.
To verify any certificate: Finance > Verify Certificate (or open /verify-certificate).
Enter the certificate number manually OR scan the QR code on the printed PDF with a phone camera.
The verification page shows whether the certificate is valid and displays project and contractor details.
Use Funding Sources Report, Project Finance Overview, and Pending Bills for deeper analysis.
tips: Verify certificates before relying on printed or shared copies.
Verification works without logging in — citizens and contractors can use the public page.
Logged-in staff see a link to open the related project from verification results.

moduleGuides.title: 12. Reports, report library, and scheduling
moduleGuides.purpose: Find built-in reports, export official outputs, and schedule recurring reports.
steps: Open Reports > Reports hub — searchable index of all report screens.
Choose the report (APR, County Operations, Status, Absorption, Pending Bills, Yearly Trends, PMC, etc.).
Set filters (department, financial year, status, ward, dates).
Review totals and exceptions before exporting.
Save approved outputs to Report Library where applicable.
Use Scheduled Reports for recurring email distribution.
Built-in reports are separate from AI Professional Reports — use Reports hub for standard county templates.
tips: Record filter criteria when sharing exported reports.
Reports are only as accurate as underlying project, finance, and monitoring data.

moduleGuides.title: 13. Public dashboard and citizen engagement
moduleGuides.purpose: Manage what citizens see and how they engage.
steps: Review the public dashboard URL and approved public projects.
Use Public Approval to approve projects for citizen visibility.
Moderate feedback and manage announcements.
Only approved content should appear on public pages.
tips: Avoid publishing sensitive internal notes.
Coordinate sensitive responses with the responsible department.

moduleGuides.title: 14. User administration and mobile app releases
moduleGuides.purpose: Manage users, roles, scope, OTP, mobile app releases, and AI usage.
steps: Open Admin > User Management for accounts, roles, and organisation scope.
Use Admin > Mobile App Usage to review field collector adoption.
Use Admin > AI Usage (Super Admin) for assistant cost and activity.
Staff download the Android collector from Dashboard > Mobile app or /mobile-app when signed in.
tips: Use least privilege for roles and scope.
Document administrator actions for audit.

moduleGuides.title: 15. Metadata, workflows, and audit trail
moduleGuides.purpose: Maintain master data, approval levels, workflows, and investigate audit logs.
steps: Open Admin > Metadata Management or relevant setup pages.
Search before adding duplicate metadata values.
Use Audit Trail to investigate user actions and support incidents.
Test workflow changes before rolling out to users.
tips: Duplicate metadata causes reporting errors.
Use audit trail facts when investigating incidents.

quickTasks: Search by name/code or filter by department, status, ward, or financial year.
Projects > Project Status or Project Details
Open the project, add status/progress, attach evidence.
Complete BQ where required, create interim/final certificate, generate PDF with QR code.
Enter certificate number OR scan QR on the PDF. Public URL: /verify-certificate?cert=NUMBER
Search for the report, set filters, review totals, export.
Open the relevant dashboard first, then Generate Professional Report → choose type, Word/PDF.
Find project, add visit/checklist notes, save observations.
Filter ward/period, complete ward PMC entries.
Create/edit templates; record visit submissions on web or mobile.
Download APK while signed in; install on Android; sign in and sync checklists.
Download template, preview, fix errors, confirm import.
troubleshootingRows: Cannot log in
Wrong password, inactive account, pending approval, OTP issue.
Confirm username, account status, password reset. Escalate OTP failures to ICT.
Missing menu item
Role or organisation scope does not include the module.
Check role, privileges, and organisation scope.
Cannot see expected project
Active filters or scope excludes the project.
Clear filters, verify department and user scope.
Dashboard figures look wrong
Filters differ or source data incomplete.
Record filters, compare with Projects Registry.
AI gives generic answers
Question is about navigation, or screen has no live data context.
Open the relevant page first; for how-to questions see Help & Support. Ask again on the correct dashboard.
AI reports look the same on different dashboards
Report generated without opening the target screen first.
Navigate to the specific dashboard, then use Generate Professional Report.
Certificate not verified
Wrong number, voided certificate, or typo.
Re-enter number exactly as on PDF; try scanning QR code instead.
QR scan does not open verify page
Camera app blocked or wrong QR.
Manually open Finance > Verify Certificate and type the certificate number from the PDF header.
Mobile app shows offline mode
Network issue or sync failed after login.
Check connectivity, pull down to sync on Checklists tab, or reinstall latest APK from /mobile-app.
Import failed
Template errors or invalid metadata.
Review preview errors, correct spreadsheet, upload again.
Authorization error
Signed in but missing privilege.
Capture module/action; ask administrator to review permissions.
roleGuidance: Use dashboards, Reports hub, and AI summaries for status, finance, regional distribution, and attention projects.
Maintain projects, progress, evidence, department reports, and ADP implementation.
Maintain CIDP/ADP, RRI, budget traceability, catalogues, and programme alignment.
Monitoring visits, PMC ward reports, checklists, evaluation workbench, stakeholder feedback.
Finance dashboard, payments, certificates, verification, pending bills, finance overview.
Procurement stages, budget intake, contractor registry, procured projects.
Mobile app: sync checklists, record visits offline, upload when connected.
Users, roles, metadata, audit, mobile releases, AI usage monitoring.
goodPracticeItems: Search before creating duplicate projects, users, or metadata values.
Check active filters before reporting missing data.
Verify payment certificates using Finance > Verify Certificate or the QR code on the PDF.
Open the correct dashboard before generating AI professional reports.
Review AI output before official submission.
Use clear comments on progress, monitoring, procurement, and finance records.
Attach evidence where the process requires proof.
Use least privilege for roles and organisation scope.
Do not share passwords, OTPs, or confidential citizen data in screenshots.
supportChecklist: Your name, department, role, and contact.
Exact module or page (route URL if possible).
Project name/code, certificate number, report name, or user account involved.
Action attempted and exact error message.
Screenshot with passwords and personal data hidden.
Date/time and whether issue affects one user or many.

aiAssistantGuide.title: AI Assistant and professional reports
aiAssistantGuide.summary: The sparkle AI button (bottom-right) answers questions using live data for your account and this Help manual for navigation. Professional reports are generated from the screen you are currently viewing.
steps: Navigate to the dashboard, project, or report screen that matches your question.
Open AI Assistant and ask a question or click Generate Professional Report.
Choose report type and Word/PDF — finance dashboards favour Finance Summary; monitoring screens favour Monitoring Summary.
Review the download before official use.
tips: For how-to questions (verify certificate, find a menu), the assistant should cite exact menu paths from Help & Support.
Super Admins monitor usage at Admin > AI Usage.

dashboardGuides.title: Finance Dashboard
dashboardGuides.purpose: Budget allocation, disbursement, absorption, and finance KPIs for accessible projects.
dashboardGuides.aiReportHint: Generate a Finance Summary professional report while on this dashboard.

dashboardGuides.title: Operations Dashboard
dashboardGuides.purpose: Operational delivery metrics and attention indicators across departments.

dashboardGuides.title: Project By Status Dashboard
dashboardGuides.purpose: Project counts and charts grouped by implementation status.
dashboardGuides.aiReportHint: Generate a Project Status professional report while on this dashboard.

dashboardGuides.title: Regional Breakdown Dashboard
dashboardGuides.purpose: Subcounty and ward distribution of projects and budgets.

dashboardGuides.title: Summary Statistics
dashboardGuides.purpose: County-wide summary cards and high-level trends.

dashboardGuides.title: Status Report
dashboardGuides.purpose: Tabular project status export with filters.
dashboardGuides.aiReportHint: AI Project Status reports use live data from this filtered view.

dashboardGuides.title: Pending Bills Report
dashboardGuides.aiReportHint: AI Finance Summary reports from this screen focus on pending bills.

navigationTopics.title: Verify a payment certificate
navigationTopics.summary: Confirm whether a Machakos County payment certificate is genuine. Works for staff and the public without login.
steps: Open Finance → Verify Certificate in the ribbon (or go to /verify-certificate).
Option A — Scan: use your phone camera on the QR code printed on the certificate PDF (labelled Scan to verify this certificate).
Option B — Manual: type the certificate number exactly as shown on the PDF header.
Press Verify and read the result — valid certificates show project name, contractor, dates, and status.
Staff users can click through to open the related project record.
tips: QR links open /verify-certificate?cert=CERTIFICATE_NUMBER automatically.
If verification fails, check for typos or confirm the certificate was generated in this system.

navigationTopics.title: Create and manage payment certificates
navigationTopics.menuPath: Finance → Payment Certificates; or Project Details → Certificates tab
navigationTopics.summary: Staff create interim/final certificates per project; PDFs include signatures and a verification QR code.
steps: Open the project in Projects Registry → Certificates tab (or Finance → Payment Certificates for the county list).
Complete bill of quantities requirements where configured.
Create interim or final certificate with correct amounts and references.
Run approval workflow if required.
Generate PDF — the QR code on the PDF links to the public verify page.
tips: Always verify issued PDFs using Finance → Verify Certificate before external sharing.

navigationTopics.title: Generate a professional AI report
navigationTopics.menuPath: AI Assistant button (bottom-right on any page)
navigationTopics.summary: Downloadable Word/PDF reports built from live data on the screen you are viewing.
steps: Navigate to the dashboard or report screen that matches your topic (finance dashboard for finance, monitoring for monitoring, etc.).
Click the sparkle AI Assistant button at the bottom-right.
Click Generate Professional Report OR ask in chat for a well formatted report.
Select report type (Project Status, Finance Summary, CIDP Linkage, Monitoring Summary, General M&E).
Choose Word (.docx) or PDF and add specific instructions.
Download and review before official submission.
tips: Reports differ by screen — open Finance Dashboard before a finance report, Status Report before a status report, etc.
Chat summaries and professional reports use different outputs — use Generate Professional Report for downloadable documents.

navigationTopics.title: Mobile Field Collector (Android)
navigationTopics.summary: Android app for offline checklist data collection in the field.
steps: Sign in to the web system and open Dashboard → Mobile app (Android) or /mobile-app.
Download and install the published APK.
Open Machakos Collector, sign in with county credentials.
On Checklists tab, pull down to sync templates and projects.
Tap a checklist, select project, complete form, submit (queues offline if needed).
tips: Install the latest published version when prompted.
Pull down on Checklists to retry sync if offline mode appears.

navigationTopics.title: Checklists and monitoring visits
navigationTopics.summary: Web tool to build checklist templates and record visit submissions; syncs with the mobile collector.
steps: Open Monitoring → Checklists & visits.
Create or edit checklist templates with sections and questions.
Record visit submissions linked to projects.
Export visit PDFs where available.
Field teams can use the mobile app for offline collection.
tips: Template changes apply to new visits — coordinate updates with field teams.

navigationTopics.title: Find the right built-in report
navigationTopics.summary: Searchable index of all standard county reports (separate from AI professional reports).
steps: Open Reports → Reports hub.
Search by report name or keyword.
Open the report card, set filters, generate/export.
Use Report Library to archive approved outputs.
Use Scheduled Reports for recurring email delivery.
tips: Built-in reports use county templates; AI professional reports use the sparkle assistant.

navigationTopics.title: PMC ward reports
steps: Open Monitoring → PMC Ward Reports.
Filter by ward, period, or financial year as required.
Complete or review ward PMC entries.
Export or submit according to county procedure.

navigationTopics.title: Finance Dashboard
navigationTopics.summary: High-level finance KPIs for projects in your scope.
steps: Open Dashboard → Finance Dashboard.
Set department, financial year, and status filters.
Review summary cards and charts.
Use AI Assistant here for finance summaries or Finance Summary professional reports.
